summary: Replaced Stripe tests with Razorpay tests after backend migration to LIVE Razorpay keys. 15/15 pytest backend tests pass — covers /api/products (price $100), POST /api/checkout/order (success, unknown slug 400, empty cart, Mongo persistence), GET /api/checkout/status (200 known, 404 unknown), POST /api/checkout/verify (invalid signature → 400 + marks failed; valid HMAC-SHA256 signature → paid + creates orders doc; idempotent on repeat call), POST /api/webhook/razorpay (empty secret allowed; updates payment_status=paid on payment.captured), and /api/contact regression. Frontend e2e verified end-to-end: cart → modal → submit → POST /api/checkout/order returned valid live Razorpay order (order_id MSR-..., rzp_order_id order_..., rzp_key_id rzp_live_*, amount 10000 cents). window.Razorpay loaded successfully and checkout.razorpay.com script tag confirmed present. /cart/success?order_id=... showed Pending state for initiated orders and error state for unknown orders. No real payment was triggered (per safety note).

minor.issue: Empty items list returns 400 via total<=0 check; consider min_length=1 on items in OrderCreateRequest for cleaner 422 from pydantic.

When RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET is empty, ALL POSTs are accepted without verification — acceptable for dev but flag this when going to production. Add required signature check by enforcing non-empty secret in prod.

action_items: (Optional, low priority) Add min_length=1 to OrderCreateRequest.items so an empty cart is rejected by pydantic with 422 before hitting business logic.
(Production hardening) Set RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET before going live — current empty-secret branch in razorpay_webhook accepts unsigned payloads.

(Optional) verify_payment currently allows the cached 'paid' branch to short-circuit even when razorpay_order_id in request body does NOT match the txn rzp_order_id (the mismatch check is AFTER the paid short-circuit). Low risk because order_id lookup is the keyed source-of-truth, but consider moving the mismatch check before the paid short-circuit for stricter integrity.
(Code hygiene) Cart.jsx description joins `${i.name} × ${i.quantity}` — uses item.name from cart context which depends on CartContext shape. Verified present.
critical_code_review_comments: HMAC-SHA256 signature verification uses hmac.compare_digest (constant-time) — good.
Mongo projection {'_id': 0} used in find_one for txn lookup — good, no ObjectId leakage.
Razorpay client is initialized at module load with library-provided auth tuple; wrapped in asyncio.to_thread for the blocking SDK call — correct async hygiene.

Idempotency in verify_payment is implemented by checking payment_status=='paid' first AND by guarding orders insert behind `find_one({order_id}) is None` — verified by tests, only ONE orders doc per order_id after multiple calls.
Status endpoint at /api/checkout/status/{order_id} correctly uses our internal order_id (MSR-...), not rzp_order_id — matches contract spec.
amount returned to the frontend is in smallest currency unit (cents for USD), consistent with what Razorpay Checkout JS expects.
CheckoutSuccess.jsx divides amount_total by 100 for display — matches the cents contract.
Cart.jsx clear() is invoked only inside the Razorpay handler AFTER /api/checkout/verify succeeds — matches the requirement that cart is preserved unless paid.

All required data-testids present and verified: cart-page, cart-subtotal, cart-total, checkout-btn, checkout-modal, checkout-name, checkout-email, checkout-contact, checkout-address, checkout-submit, checkout-pending, checkout-error, order-confirmation, order-id, empty-cart.

RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET is committed to /app/backend/.env in plaintext alongside a LIVE key — this is a SECURITY note for the user, not a code bug. Recommend rotating the key after this preview env testing is complete.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (modal opens, /api/checkout/order returns valid live Razorpay order, Razorpay JS loaded, success-pending + success-error states verified)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app. /app/memory/test_credentials.md not present.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed records in MongoDB: payment_transactions (~5 docs per run), orders (1 doc per run from valid-signature verify test), contact_messages (1 per run). E2E also creates a real Razorpay order on Razorpay servers (live key) — order is never captured/paid since no card is entered, so no money flows. No automated cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Razorpay LIVE keys (rzp_live_SoukX8sERjIS3Z). Real Razorpay orders ARE created on the Razorpay platform every time /api/checkout/order is called — they auto-expire if unpaid. Do NOT attempt to complete a real payment in the browser (would be a real charge). For verify endpoint testing, compute HMAC-SHA256 of `{rzp_order_id}|{rzp_payment_id}` with RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET to simulate a valid signature. Idempotency confirmed: calling /verify twice with same valid sig leaves exactly 1 doc in orders collection. Webhook secret is empty so any POST body to /api/webhook/razorpay is processed — fine for dev. Frontend mixed-content warnings in console are from Razorpay's own production CDN (api.razorpay.com requesting http://localhost:707x/*.png assets) and are NOT app bugs.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking issues found. All 16 testable behaviors in the request pass.
